opp.next_step: 2/3 - They wont be ready to evaluate till September. Qualifying out
opp.ais_why:
• Next step explicitly says the prospect won't be ready to evaluate until September and the rep is qualifying out — no AI interest expressed.
• SF description simply says "Revisit in September 2026" and AIS is null — no prospect-side AI cue anywhere on record.
• No Avoma calls on file. Public-sector buyer (Pima County) is effectively parked with no Merlin, ANA or intake mention.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-01-21, Bruce Collins: 'We have most of it is broken up into 3 different systems from the online bidding system that we're currently using, Bitnet, then we have Workday, and then we have Onbase as our system of record. So we have 3 different contracts, 3 different vendors, all within the last 16 months.'
Avoma 2026-01-21, Bruce: 'All of it is relevant.' (when asked if the Zycus suite addresses Pima County needs)
metrics.narrative: Bruce Collins articulated the business problem on 2026-01-21: procurement processes are fragmented across three separate platforms (Bitnet for online bidding, Workday ERP, and Onbase as system of record), causing inefficiencies and contract-management complexity (three different vendors, three contracts). He stated 'all of it is relevant' when shown the full Zycus suite and specifically highlighted the need for a single unified procurement platform to replace the three-system setup. However, no quantified impact was stated (no dollar savings target, no cycle-time reduction, no headcount efficiency, no error-rate metric). The qualitative pain is clear and confirmed; quantifying it (cost of managing three vendors, time lost to system-hopping, compliance risk) is the next step. Bruce's background includes a prior role where he tracked 20,000 invoices/year with a KPI of 97% paid within 25 calendar days, indicating he understands operational metrics, but he did not volunteer quantified pain for Pima County's current state.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-01-21, Bruce Collins: 'I'm really a nerd. So when I see things in magazines and online, I always ask the question of, can there be value for my organization to look into that so we can learn best practices.'
Avoma 2026-01-21, Bruce: 'I love the black ring.' (Merlin AI)

Avoma 2026-01-21, Bruce: 'Historically, if I've seen something that I like but my organization may not be in a position to buy, I will forward it to one of my peers... and say, hey. We can't afford this, but you guys need to take a look at it.'

Avoma 2026-01-21, Bruce: 'How about setting up the Sharepoint? ... I would like our IT director to see something in Sharepoint. Our CFO... if there's something in the Sharepoint site that I could point him to.'
No evidence of Bruce activating the CFO or IT director in the 5 months since the call; no follow-up meeting scheduled; deal deferred to September 2026 per Next Step 2026-02-03.
champion.narrative: Bruce Collins (Pima County procurement lead, continuous improvement green belt) is a developing champion. On the 2026-01-21 discovery call he demonstrated strong personal interest ('I'm really a nerd... I love the black ring '), understood the value proposition (platform consolidation, AI capabilities, quick time-to-value vs Workday), and committed to an internal advocacy action: sharing the Zycus SharePoint site with the IT director and CFO, and forwarding promising solutions to procurement peers or municipal leaders if Pima County cannot adopt. However, Bruce has no budget authority (reports to a deputy county administrator, not the CFO or county executive), has not yet activated the Economic Buyer or IT director (no follow-through evidence in the 5 months since the call), and explicitly deferred evaluation to September 2026 due to CFO bandwidth constraints. Bruce's advocacy is passive at this stage: he is willing to forward materials but has not driven urgency, secured a next meeting, or introduced Zycus to the decision-makers. Champion strength is 'developing' because Bruce is engaged and sees the value, but has not yet demonstrated access to or influence over the Economic Buyer, and the deal has been on hold for 5 months with no forward motion. Reactivating Bruce and converting him to an active champion (securing the CFO/IT director meeting in September 2026) is the critical next step.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-01-21, Bruce Collins: 'We have most of it is broken up into 3 different systems... Bitnet, Workday, and Onbase... 3 different contracts, 3 different vendors, all within the last 16 months.'
Avoma 2026-01-21, Bruce: 'Pima County has implemented recently Workday... we're still letting Workday settle... we're still working through having spent quite a bit of money adjusting to Workday.'
SF Competitors__c: blank; no named vendor competitors in any source.
competition.narrative: The primary competitor is 'Do Nothing' (status quo). Bruce Collins stated on 2026-01-21 that Pima County currently uses three separate platforms: Bitnet for online bidding, Workday ERP, and Onbase as the system of record, all implemented within the last 16 months. The three-vendor setup is the incumbent alternative. No other named vendor competitors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer, etc.) were mentioned on the call or in Salesforce fields. The competitive wedge is platform consolidation and AI-driven automation (Merlin): Bruce expressed strong interest in the 'black ring' (Merlin agentic platform) and the ability to replace three systems with one unified source-to-pay platform, with quick time-to-value (12-16 weeks vs Workday's multi-year burden). However, the status quo is heavily entrenched: Pima County just spent significant money on Workday (16 months ago, still stabilizing) and has change fatigue ('we're still letting Workday settle'). The CFO has no capacity due to budget cycles, and the buyer explicitly deferred evaluation to September 2026. The 'do nothing' risk is high because the pain is real but not yet urgent enough to override the Workday stabilization priority. Zycus must position as a complementary, low-disruption procurement layer that integrates with Workday (not a replacement) and delivers quick ROI without the heavy-lift ERP pain Bruce wants to avoid.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-01-21, Bruce Collins: 'We have most of it is broken up into 3 different systems from the online bidding system that we're currently using, Bitnet, then we have Workday, and then we have Onbase as our system of record. So we have 3 different contracts, 3 different vendors, all within the last 16 months.'

Avoma 2026-01-21, Bruce: 'Pima County has implemented recently Workday... maybe 16 months ago. So we're still letting Workday settle... we're still working through having spent quite a bit of money adjusting to Workday.'
Avoma 2026-01-21, Bruce: 'All of it is relevant.' (when asked if the Zycus suite addresses the pain)
identify_pain.narrative: Bruce Collins, the procurement lead (green belt continuous improvement), explicitly stated the operational pain on 2026-01-21: Pima County's procurement processes are fragmented across three separate platforms (Bitnet for online bidding, Workday ERP, and Onbase as system of record), requiring management of three different vendors and three contracts, all implemented within the last 16 months. He described this as causing inefficiencies and stated 'all of it is relevant' when shown the full Zycus suite, indicating the pain of platform fragmentation is acute and the need for consolidation is clear. Bruce also highlighted the Workday implementation burden: 'we're still letting Workday settle... we're still working through having spent quite a bit of money adjusting to Workday,' indicating change fatigue and a desire to avoid another heavy-lift ERP-style project. The pain owner is Bruce Collins (procurement lead), with the CFO (finance director) as the budget authority who must approve any new investment. Bruce's role as a continuous-improvement advocate ('I'm really a nerd... I gotta learn') positions him as the internal sponsor, but the pain is shared across procurement, finance, and IT.

sources: No evidence in the 2026-01-21 call or Salesforce fields; gap to be closed during formal evaluation phase.
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec/security review) were discussed on the 2026-01-21 discovery call. No mention of Pima County's procurement policies, approval thresholds, or legal requirements. No discussion of implementation scoping, SI/partner involvement, or ERP integration requirements (though Workday integration would be critical). The paper process is a genuine knowledge gap at this early Qualified stage. Once the CFO and IT director review the materials and the deal advances to active evaluation (deferred to September 2026), the paper process will need to be mapped, including Workday integration, InfoSec review (common for government entities), and any cooperative purchasing or sole-source justification requirements for a public-sector procurement.

So I'll report to one of the deputy county administrators. But where I'm connected at the hip with the finance director that serves as our CFO.'

Avoma 2026-01-21, Bruce: 'Our CFO, right now, we're starting the budget. He's not gonna look at anything. He has 34 budgets to review to prepare to present to the board, and we're just starting next week for every department to present. So he's not gonna have capacity. But if there's something in the SharePoint site that I could point him to.'
No named CFO or IT director; no contact roles on the opportunity; no EB participation in calls.
economic_buyer.narrative: Bruce Collins identified the approval chain on 2026-01-21: Pima County has a county executive, two deputy county administrators, and department heads. Bruce reports to one of the deputy county administrators and is 'connected at the hip' with the finance director who serves as the CFO. Budget authority sits with the CFO (the finance director), who Bruce stated was reviewing 34 departmental budgets at the time of the call and had no capacity to evaluate new solutions immediately. The CFO is the Economic Buyer, but has not been named, contacted, or engaged. Bruce is the internal champion/coach who will forward materials to the CFO and the IT director for review via the SharePoint site Zycus committed to set up. Bruce's last statement on 2026-01-21 was that he will share the SharePoint link with the IT director and CFO once received, and will forward promising solutions to procurement peers or municipal leaders if Pima County cannot adopt. The EB is identified by role but not by name, has not participated in any call, and no direct EB engagement has occurred. Activating the CFO and IT director is the critical next step.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-01-21, Bruce Collins: 'How about setting up the Sharepoint? Because what I will, I would like our IT director to see something in Sharepoint. Our CFO, right now, we're starting the budget. He's not gonna look at anything... But if there's something in the Sharepoint site that I could point him to.'
SF Next_Step__c updated 2026-02-03: '2/3 - They wont be ready to evaluate till September. Qualifying out'
No formal vendor selection process, RFP timeline, or evaluation committee mentioned on the call.
decision_process.narrative: Bruce Collins outlined a multi-step internal process on 2026-01-21: (1) Zycus to set up a SharePoint site with the discovery call recording, collateral, and mini demo videos (including CLM); (2) Bruce will share the SharePoint link with the IT director and the CFO for review after the budget cycle; (3) Bruce will build internal consensus; (4) if consensus is reached, Bruce will come back to Zycus and request a formal demo and business case support to justify budget to the deputy county administrator and CFO. Bruce explicitly stated the CFO has no capacity until after the budget presentation cycle (34 departmental budgets being reviewed, starting the week of 2026-01-27). No formal RFP or vendor selection process was mentioned. No timeline was given for CFO/IT director review, and no commitment was made to a next meeting. The owner's Next Step dated 2026-02-03 reads 'They wont be ready to evaluate till September. Qualifying out,' indicating the buyer pushed evaluation start to September 2026 (10 weeks from today, 2026-06-26). The decision process is CFO-gated and currently on hold.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-01-21, Bruce Collins: 'All of it is relevant. We have most of it is broken up into 3 different systems...'
Avoma 2026-01-21, Bruce: 'I love the black ring.' (referring to Merlin AI capabilities)

Avoma 2026-01-21, Bruce: 'I'm really a nerd. So when I see things in magazines and online, I always ask the question of, can there be value for my organization to look into that so we can learn best practices and explore what we don't know and how we can improve as a green belt is I'm still in continuous improvement.'
decision_criteria.narrative: The only stated decision criterion on 2026-01-21 was platform consolidation: Bruce said 'all of it is relevant' and highlighted the need to replace the three-system setup (Bitnet, Workday, Onbase) with a single unified procurement platform. He expressed strong interest in Merlin's AI capabilities ('I love the black ring') and in learning best practices ('I'm really a nerd... can there be value for my organization to look into that so we can learn best practices'). He referenced a prior success at City of El Paso with a cooperative contracts marketplace, indicating openness to innovative procurement solutions. However, no formal evaluation criteria, RFP requirements, or weighted scoring framework have been documented. No discussion of fit criteria (must-haves vs nice-to-haves), no mention of security/InfoSec requirements, integration requirements, or vendor selection process. The decision criteria are inferred from the pain (consolidation, AI, ease of use) but not explicitly stated by the buyer. A formal evaluation (which Bruce deferred to September 2026) will surface the criteria.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Bruce Collins demonstrated 'AI Curious' to 'AI Hungry' signal on the 2026-01-21 discovery call. He explicitly stated 'I love the black ring' (Merlin AI) and positioned himself as a continuous improvement advocate interested in learning best practices and exploring what the organization doesn't know. His prior success with an innovative cooperative contracts marketplace at City of El Paso indicates openness to cutting-edge procurement solutions. However, no AIS score is recorded in Salesforce, and the CFO's AI appetite is unknown (CFO has not been contacted). The buyer's Workday burden and desire for quick time-to-value (12-16 weeks) vs heavy-lift ERP pain suggests receptivity to AI-driven automation as a low-disruption efficiency lever. Tier: AI Curious (Bruce is intrigued and sees value, but AI appetite at the power level is unconfirmed and no AI-specific use case has been scoped).

items.change: Discovery call with Bruce Collins (Pima County procurement); strong interest in full suite and Merlin AI; pain confirmed (3-system fragmentation); Zycus committed to SharePoint site; Bruce to share with CFO and IT director.

Owner Next Step updated: 'They wont be ready to evaluate till September. Qualifying out.' Buyer deferred evaluation start to September 2026 due to CFO bandwidth (budget cycle) and Workday stabilization priority.
LastActivityDate (rep-side touch, likely a logged task/note; no buyer engagement).

deal_movement.summary: Single discovery call on 2026-01-21 (~22 weeks ago, or 5 months ago) where Bruce Collins (Pima County procurement lead) expressed strong interest in the full Zycus suite (all modules relevant, especially Merlin AI) and the value proposition of consolidating three fragmented systems (Bitnet, Workday, Onbase) into one platform. Zycus committed to set up a SharePoint site with the call recording, collateral, and mini demo videos for Bruce to share with the CFO and IT director. Bruce stated the CFO had no capacity to evaluate due to budget cycle (34 departmental budgets under review) and deferred evaluation to a later time. Owner Next Step updated 2026-02-03 (~20 weeks ago) reads 'They wont be ready to evaluate till September. Qualifying out,' indicating buyer pushed evaluation start to September 2026 (10 weeks from today). No subsequent buyer-facing activity, no follow-up call, no SharePoint access confirmation, no CFO or IT director engagement. LastActivityDate 2026-06-19 (7 days ago) is a rep-side touch (likely a logged note or task), not buyer engagement. Deal is stalled at early Qualified stage with the buyer explicitly on hold until September 2026.

items.detail: Buyer explicitly deferred evaluation to September 2026 (10 weeks from today, 2026-06-26). If evaluation starts on schedule in early September, the remaining runway to the February 2027 close date is approximately 5 months (150 days). For a large enterprise deal requiring discovery, RFI/RFP, shortlist, demos, workshops, commercials, negotiation, InfoSec review, and contracting, 5 months is aggressive. Any slip in the September start date (e.g., CFO bandwidth, Workday stabilization delays) will push the close date beyond February 2027. The close date is already at risk due to the deferred evaluation start.
items.status: Unresolved; buyer on hold until September 2026; no mitigation plan in place.

items.detail: Economic Buyer (CFO, finance director) and IT director have not been contacted or engaged. Zero buyer-side contact roles on the opportunity. Bruce Collins (procurement lead, no budget authority) is the sole contact and has not activated the power players in 5 months. No CFO sponsorship, no IT director buy-in, no executive alignment. This is a single-threaded deal with a non-decision-maker, creating high ghost risk and low win probability unless multi-threading succeeds in September 2026.
items.status: Unresolved; CFO and IT director engagement is the critical path and has not occurred.

items.detail: CFO is currently reviewing 34 departmental budgets for presentation to the board and has no capacity to evaluate new solutions until after the budget cycle (deferred to post-September 2026). No budget allocated for a new procurement platform, and the deal opportunity amount ($150k) is not anchored to a confirmed budget line. Bruce stated Pima County 'may not be in a position to buy,' indicating budget uncertainty. The September 2026 evaluation window is post-budget-cycle, but no commitment that budget will be available has been secured.
items.status: Unresolved; no budget confirmation, no CFO buy-in, no business case quantifying ROI vs 3-vendor cost.

items.detail: Pima County implemented Workday 16 months ago (mid-2024) and is still stabilizing the system with workflow adjustments and patches. Bruce explicitly stated 'we're still letting Workday settle' and 'spent quite a bit of money adjusting to Workday,' indicating change fatigue and capacity constraints for new system rollouts. The risk is that the organization prioritizes Workday stabilization over a new procurement platform, and the status quo (Do Nothing) wins due to change aversion. Zycus must position as a low-disruption, Workday-integrated layer (not a replacement) to mitigate this risk.
items.status: Unresolved; change fatigue is real and Zycus positioning as Workday-complementary (not disruptive) has not been reinforced with CFO or IT director.

items.detail: Public-sector procurement (Pima County, Arizona) may require adherence to cooperative purchasing agreements, sole-source justification, or competitive RFP processes mandated by county policy or state law. No discussion of procurement policies, approval thresholds, or political considerations (county board, public scrutiny) occurred on the discovery call. The paper process and political landscape are unknown, creating risk that unforeseen approval gates or policy requirements could delay or derail the deal.
items.status: Unresolved; public-sector procurement requirements and political landscape not yet mapped.

champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-01-21; no follow-through evidence in 5 months; deal deferred to September 2026 per Next Step 2026-02-03
champion_strength.summary: Bruce Collins is a developing champion with strong personal interest in the Zycus suite (platform consolidation, Merlin AI) and a track record of advocating for innovative procurement solutions (referenced prior success at City of El Paso). However, Bruce has no budget authority, has not activated the Economic Buyer (CFO) or IT director in the 5 months since the discovery call, and the deal is on hold until September 2026 per his explicit deferral. Champion strength is 'developing' because Bruce is engaged and sees the value, but has not yet demonstrated the access, influence, or urgency required to drive the deal forward. Reactivating Bruce ahead of the September evaluation window and converting him to an active advocate who secures the CFO/IT director meeting is the critical next step.
champion_strength.champion: Bruce Collins (Pima County procurement lead, continuous improvement green belt)

Positions Zycus as a financially defensible investment that respects the Workday stabilization priority (low-disruption, Workday-integrated layer, not a replacement).

Demonstrates follow-through and keeps Zycus top-of-mind ahead of the evaluation window.

If Bruce cannot commit or the September window slips, this call surfaces the slip early enough to adjust the close date or qualify out.

Positions Zycus as a Workday-complementary, secure, low-risk procurement layer and increases the likelihood of IT director sponsorship alongside CFO approval.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is cooling due to 5 months of inactivity since the single discovery call on 2026-01-21. Bruce Collins (the sole contact) has not followed through on his commitment to share the Zycus SharePoint site with the CFO and IT director, and no Economic Buyer or IT director engagement has occurred. The buyer explicitly deferred evaluation to September 2026 due to CFO bandwidth constraints (budget cycle) and Workday stabilization priorities. LastActivityDate 2026-06-19 (7 days ago) is a rep-side touch, not buyer engagement. The close date (2027-02-01, 220 days out) is distant, but the deferred evaluation start leaves insufficient runway for a 12-15 month enterprise sales cycle. Confidence signals are negative: single-threaded, no power-player engagement, no follow-through on commitments, explicit deferral, and 5 months of silence. The deal is at high risk of remaining stalled or qualifying out unless Bruce is reactivated ahead of September 2026 and the CFO/IT director meeting is secured.

north_star_verdict.math: 220 days to close (2027-02-01). In Qualified stage for approximately 5 months since creation (2026-01-22). Last activity 2026-06-19 (7 days ago). Close date is distant (February 2027), but deal has been explicitly deferred by the buyer with no active evaluation underway. Buyer stated on 2026-01-21 that Workday implementation 16 months prior is still stabilizing and they will not be ready to evaluate until September 2026. Owner's Next Step dated 2026-02-03 reads 'They wont be ready to evaluate till September. Qualifying out.' No forward slip in Salesforce yet, but the deal is effectively on hold with zero evaluation activity planned for the next 10 weeks. Verdict: Slowing (not Off Track, because the relationship is still live and the deferral reason is documented and credible; but not Close Date Risk or On Track because no active deal motion is underway).
evidence: Bruce Collins (Pima County procurement, green belt continuous improvement) stated on 2026-01-21 call that Workday was implemented 16 months ago and is still stabilizing with workflow adjustments and patches coming, limiting capacity for new system rollouts.
Bruce stated on 2026-01-21 that CFO is reviewing 34 departmental budgets and has no capacity to evaluate new solutions immediately.
Owner Next Step updated 2026-02-03: 'They wont be ready to evaluate till September. Qualifying out.'
No buyer-facing activity or deliverable since the single discovery call on 2026-01-21 (5 months ago, or ~22 weeks ago).
LastActivityDate 2026-06-19 is a rep-side touch (likely a logged task/note), not buyer engagement.

Close date 2027-02-01 is 220 days out, but evaluation start is deferred to September 2026 (10 weeks from now), leaving insufficient runway for discovery, RFI/RFP, shortlist, demo, workshop, commercials, and contracting by February 2027 unless the process compresses significantly.
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains stalled in Qualified with zero buyer engagement since January 2026 discovery call. Buyer deferred evaluation to September 2026 due to Workday stabilization; that restart window has now arrived but no forward motion or re-engagement visible.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal is stalled at early Qualified stage with the buyer explicitly deferring evaluation to September 2026 (10 weeks from today, 2026-08-26 to 2026-09-02 window). No forward momentum for 5 months. The single contact (Bruce Collins) is a developing champion with no budget authority and has not activated the Economic Buyer (CFO) or IT director. The status quo (Do Nothing) is heavily entrenched due to recent Workday investment and change fatigue. Reactivating Bruce ahead of the September evaluation window and securing the CFO/IT director meeting is the critical path to advancing this deal.

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is 'Do Nothing' (status quo). Pima County is prioritizing Workday stabilization and has explicitly deferred new procurement platform evaluation to September 2026 (10 weeks from today). The three-vendor setup (Bitnet, Workday, Onbase) is the incumbent alternative, entrenched by recent investment (16 months ago) and change fatigue. No named vendor competitors. Zycus must position as a low-disruption, quick-ROI procurement layer that integrates with Workday (not a replacement) and activates the CFO and IT director when evaluation resumes in September 2026. The wedge is platform consolidation plus Merlin AI, delivered in 12-16 weeks vs multi-year ERP pain.

competitors.name: Do Nothing (Status Quo: Bitnet + Workday + Onbase)
competitors.quote: We have 3 different systems... Bitnet, Workday, and Onbase... 3 different contracts, 3 different vendors, all within the last 16 months. Pima County has implemented recently Workday... we're still letting Workday settle... we're still working through having spent quite a bit of money adjusting to Workday. Our CFO... has 34 budgets to review... he's not gonna have capacity.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as a complementary procurement layer (not an ERP replacement) that integrates with Workday, consolidates the three-vendor setup, and delivers quick ROI (12-16 weeks time-to-value) without the heavy-lift pain of Workday. Lead with Merlin AI (Bruce loves the 'black ring') as the differentiator and the platform consolidation story as the CFO business case. Reactivate Bruce in late August 2026 (ahead of the September evaluation window) with a refreshed SharePoint site, CFO-ready ROI materials, and a direct request to facilitate the CFO/IT director meeting.

items.quote: How about setting up the Sharepoint? Because what I will, I would like our IT director to see something in Sharepoint. Our CFO... if there's something in the Sharepoint site that I could point him to.
items.requirement: Set up a SharePoint site with the discovery call recording, Zycus collateral, mini demo videos, and a new CLM tool video for CFO review.

items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-01-21, Michael McCarthy commitment to Bruce Collins
items.commitment: Set up the SharePoint site containing the meeting recording, Zycus product collateral, mini demo videos (including the CLM tool video), and share the link with Bruce Collins for distribution to the IT director and CFO.
items.deliverable: Set up the SharePoint site containing the meeting recording, Zycus product collateral, mini demo videos (including the CLM tool video), and share the link with Bruce Collins for distribution to the IT director and CFO.
items.grounding_quote: Michael McCarthy (Zycus rep): 'We'll put this recording on there. We'll put a lot of collateral on there, some mini videos. We got a brand new video with our CLM tool that'll blow his mind. You know, we'll put some of that stuff up there. You can share it with anyone inside your organization.'

items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-01-21, Bruce Collins commitment
items.commitment: Review the Zycus SharePoint site materials once received and share with the IT director and CFO for internal evaluation.
items.deliverable: Review the Zycus SharePoint site materials once received and share with the IT director and CFO for internal evaluation.
items.grounding_quote: Bruce Collins: 'How about setting up the Sharepoint? ... I would like our IT director to see something in Sharepoint. Our CFO... if there's something in the Sharepoint site that I could point him to.' And: 'I will forward it to one of my peers... if my organization may not be in a position to buy.'

ai_positioning_strength.score: Strong interest from champion (Bruce), but under-positioned at Economic Buyer / IT director level; no AI-specific business case or proof point developed.
ai_positioning_strength.summary: Bruce Collins expressed strong interest in Merlin AI capabilities on the 2026-01-21 discovery call, stating 'I love the black ring' (referring to the Merlin agentic platform graphic) and highlighting his interest in learning best practices and exploring continuous improvement opportunities. The Merlin story (autonomous negotiation agents, AI-driven contract review, intake via Microsoft Teams, AP exception automation) resonated strongly with Bruce's pain (3-system fragmentation, Workday burden) and his persona (continuous improvement nerd). However, the AI positioning has not yet reached the Economic Buyer (CFO) or IT director, and no AI-specific use case, ROI quantification, or proof point has been developed. AI positioning is strong with the champion but under-positioned at the power level. The September 2026 evaluation kickoff is the opportunity to lead with Merlin as the differentiator in CFO-ready materials.

items.quote: All of it is relevant. We have most of it is broken up into 3 different systems... Bitnet, Workday, and Onbase... 3 different contracts, 3 different vendors.
items.criterion: Platform consolidation (replace 3-vendor setup with single unified source-to-pay platform)

items.quote: I love the black ring. I'm really a nerd... can there be value for my organization to look into that so we can learn best practices.
items.criterion: AI-driven automation and best practices (Merlin agentic platform)

items.quote: When we're talking about an implementation, we're talking 12 to 16 weeks, not 12 to 16 months... you'd be live by Memorial Day, not Memorial Day 28.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-01-21, Michael McCarthy (Zycus rep); Bruce's positive response and Workday pain context ('we're still letting Workday settle... spent quite a bit of money adjusting') indicate strong alignment
items.criterion: Quick time-to-value and low-disruption implementation (vs Workday multi-year pain)

items.quote: Our CFO, right now, we're starting the budget. He's not gonna look at anything. He has 34 budgets to review... So he's not gonna have capacity.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-01-21, Bruce Collins; Owner Next Step 2026-02-03: 'They wont be ready to evaluate till September. Qualifying out.'
items.criterion: CFO capacity and budget timing (evaluation deferred to September 2026)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Pima County's stated need for a single unified procurement platform to replace three fragmented systems (Bitnet, Workday, Onbase) aligns strongly with Zycus's full-suite source-to-pay offering. Bruce Collins confirmed 'all of it is relevant' and expressed strong interest in Merlin AI capabilities. The key fit criterion is low-disruption implementation: Bruce has change fatigue from the Workday project and emphasized the value of 12-16 week time-to-value vs multi-year ERP implementations. Zycus must position as a Workday-integrated procurement layer, not a replacement. The risk is timing: the CFO has no capacity until after the budget cycle (deferred to September 2026), and the February 2027 close date leaves insufficient runway if evaluation does not start on schedule in September 2026.

hard.ais_why:
• Next step explicitly says the prospect won't be ready to evaluate until September and the rep is qualifying out — no AI interest expressed.
• SF description simply says "Revisit in September 2026" and AIS is null — no prospect-side AI cue anywhere on record.
• No Avoma calls on file. Public-sector buyer (Pima County) is effectively parked with no Merlin, ANA or intake mention.
hard.next_step: 2/3 - They wont be ready to evaluate till September. Qualifying out

pulse.summary: Live: last verified Salesforce activity 7 day(s) ago (2026-06-19).

rep_outreach.note: rep reached out, awaiting buyer reply

deltas.subject: Secure multi-threading by activating the IT director alongside the CFO in the September 2026 evaluation kickoff. The IT director is critical for Workday integration scoping and InfoSec/security requirements (government entity). A single-threaded deal with a non-decision-maker (Bruce) is a ghost risk; the September window is the one opportunity to break through to the power players before the February 2027 close date becomes indefensible.

deltas.subject: Buyer explicitly deferred evaluation to September 2026 (10 weeks from today, 2026-06-26). If evaluation starts on schedule in early September, the remaining runway to the February 2027 close date is approximately 5 months (150 days). For a large enterprise deal requiring discovery, RFI/RFP, shortlist, demos, workshops, commercials, negotiation, InfoSec review, and contracting, 5 months is aggressive. The close date is already at risk due to the deferred evaluation start.

Paper process (MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec/security requirements, Workday integration mechanics, public-sector procurement policies) not discussed and unknown.
